All works

Air
Cooler

A residential air cooler redesigned around the idea of quiet presence — something you want in your room, not just something you tolerate in it.

Year
2023
Disciplines
Industrial Design CMF 3D Modelling Rendering
Tools
Rhinoceros 3D Keyshot Figma
Overview

The Project

Most coolers are designed purely for function — large, utilitarian, and visually aggressive. This project asks a simple question: what if the cooler was something you actually wanted in your room?

The arched form draws from domestic objects — lamps, speakers, ceramics — rather than from appliances. It is designed to sit on the floor the way a piece of furniture does: with presence, not apology.

Terracotta and forest green — the two primary colourways in context

Challenge

The Problem

Air coolers are mechanically constrained — large water tanks, grilles, and fans dictate most of the form. The challenge was to find softness and desirability within those hard constraints, without compromising thermal performance.

The arched grille geometry emerged as a key move: it references the product's airflow function directly while giving the front face a calm, architectural quality rather than a mechanical one.

Form study — proportions and grille geometry from two angles

Concept sketches — form ideation, arch proportions, and dimensioned front view

Process

How I Got There

The design process began with a teardown analysis of existing products to understand which components were fixed and which offered formal freedom. The housing was developed through sketch iterations and 3D modelling, exploring different arch proportions, grille depths, and handle treatments.

CMF was developed in parallel across four directions — terracotta, sage green, powder blue, and charcoal — each tested against the form to ensure the colour felt integral, not applied. Warm neutrals were consistently preferred over conventional white or grey.
